issues: stark white body creates slight pasted-in look against dark dungeon palette
lower body lacks pixel texture detail compared to surrounding tiles
high brightness contrast may read as unfinished rather than styled
reasoning: The barrier_closed_v02 is immediately locatable: its tall white silhouette with ornate colorful crown (teal/gold/blue accents) stands out sharply against both the dark red dungeon tiles to its left and the dark gray stone tiles to its right. Spottable well within 0.5s. Silhouette is clear and gate-like, no confusion with enemy or player entities. Dark-area visibility is strong due to high luminance. The ornate top matches Echoes pixel-art energy and adds visual character. However, the plain stark-white lower body lacks the texture detail present in surrounding environment tiles, giving a slight composited/pasted feel. The contrast is functionally good for a gameplay barrier but aesthetically the white reads slightly foreign in the dungeon color space. Marginal: readable and functional, but the body texture and tonal integration need refinement to feel fully native.
